classObjective: Write the equation of an absolute value function given its graph and model real-world situations using absolute value functions.
workedExamples: 4-7, Example 2 — Write the Equation from a Graph (continued)
4-7, Example 6 — Model Real-World Situations with Absolute Value Functions (Tolerance and Target-Value Contexts)
warmUp: Given a vertex at (1, 4) and point (3, 0), write the absolute value equation
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: using a second point to find parameter a; tolerance and target-value contexts; writing equations from graphs
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice writing absolute value equations from graphs and modeling real-world tolerance situations
independentPractice: Practice problems writing absolute value equations from graphs and applying to tolerance/target-value models
assessment: Exit ticket: write the absolute value equation for a graph with vertex (-2, 5) passing through (1, -3)
capReflection: How does solving for the parameter 'a' in |x - h| + k allow you to write an equation from a graph?
